subject: english
topic_title: Punctuation: Full Stop, Comma, Dash, Colon, Semicolon, and Quotation Marks
learning_objectives: State the uses of the punctuation marks covered
Apply each mark in sample sentences
Recognise punctuation in statements, lists, and direct speech

sections.heading: Full Stop and Comma
sections.content: A full stop marks the end of a complete statement and may also appear in abbreviations, initials, and numbering. The comma is used for separation in lists and direct speech, and to enclose appositives or introductory elements.

sections.heading: Dash and Colon
sections.content: The dash indicates a break in sense or faltering speech. The colon announces a list, explanation, or long quotation.

sections.heading: Quotation Marks
sections.content: Quotation marks are used for direct speech, direct quotations from authorities, and titles of short works such as poems, articles, and songs.
